content.claim: A separate international comparative study using PISA data found no significant decrease in correlations between student socioeconomic status and results in PISA domains after Hungarian educational reform affecting tracking, contradicting the Horn/TARKI finding and suggesting reform did not measurably worsen SES-outcome links.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: PISA and micro-region competence-test methodologies may capture different outcome dimensions (e.g., standardized vs. curriculum-aligned assessment)
Timing of PISA survey relative to reform implementation and sample composition not documented
International PISA comparability versus Hungarian-specific institutional context may explain divergent findings
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
